Registry / Terms / weighted mean error (WME)

weighted mean error (WME)

—Not in VIM/VIML G 18 #02089 · 4 instances

Suggested actions

  1. HIGH No authoritative definition in VIM or VIML.
  2. MEDIUM 4 distinct definitions — harmonise.

Publication instances

Ed.YearPublicationClauseDefinitionConsistency
20102007OIML R 140:2007
#02089
T.2.20
weighted combination of errors of a meter or a metering module. When the specified measuring range of a The WME is used to adjust the error curve as close as possible meter to zero known The WME is calculated as follows: small measu recom adjus range accor where: - n greater than or equal to 6 being the number of measurements i performed at different flowrates Qi; - ki being the weighting factors; - Ei being the error at the flowrate Qi. For each flowrate, ki = Qi/Qmax, except for Qi = Qmax for which the weighting factor is equal to 0.4.
pending
20102006OIML R 137-1:2006
#01944
2.2.8
the weighted mean error (WME) is calculated as follows: where: • Qi/Qmax is a weighting factor; • ei is the error at the flowrate Qi; • at Qi > 0.9·Qmax a weighting factor of 0.4 shall be used instead of 1.
pending
202X2012OIML R137:2012
#02661
3.2.5
the weighted mean error (WME) within the scope of this Recommendation is defined as: withwith with with for Qi 0.7 Qmax for Qi 0.7 Qmax for Qi 0.7 Qmax for Qi 0.7 Qmax for 0.7 Qmax< Qi Qmaxfor 0.7 Qmax< Qi Qmax for 0.7 Qmax< Qi Qmax for 0.7 Qmax< Qi Qmax where: ki = weighting factor at the flow rate Qi; Ei = the error at the flow rate Qi.
pending
202X2007OIML R 140:2007
#02089
T.2.20
weighted combination of errors of a meter or a metering module. The WME is used to adjust the error curve as close as possible to zero The WME is calculated as follows: where: n greater than or equal to 6 being the number of measurements i performed at different flowrates Qi; ki being the weighting factors; Ei being the error at the flowrate Qi. For each flowrate, ki = Qi/Qmax, except for Qi = Qmax for which the weighting factor is equal to 0.4.
pending
